channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 men convicted of charges related to human smuggling after scheme led to an Indian family’s death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas senior couple in refund limbo after Amazon purchase
item.description: When the couple needed to return a chair with missing parts, the shipping fee was more than the cost.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, NewsNation

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-backed Derek Merrin concedes to Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur in Ohio
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur’s Trump-endorsed rival said he called the veteran Ohio congresswoman to concede defeat in the 2024 election. In a video posted to X, formerly Twitter, fourth-term state Rep.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Northern California gets record rain and heavy snow. Many have been in the dark for days in Seattle
item.description: FORESTVILLE, Calif. (AP) — A major storm continued to drop heavy snow and record rain Friday as it moved through Northern California, closing roads and prompting evacuations in some areas, after killing two people and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands in the Pacific Northwest.
item.dc:creator: GODOFREDO A. VÁSQUEZ, JANIE HAR and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KREX 5

item.title: Trump-backed Derek Merrin concedes to Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur in Ohio
item.description: Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur’s Trump-endorsed rival said he called the veteran Ohio congresswoman to concede defeat in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspect in Young Dolph case placed on a 6 year diversion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- The alleged mastermind behind the killing of Memphis rapper Young Dolph and the man charged with accessory after the fact faced a judge Friday morning. Hernandez Govan is facing charges of attempted and first-degree murder plus conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Alvin Bragg's prosecutors pivot to nailing Danny Penny, cross line in court
item.description: Expert witness for the defense, Dr. Satish Chundru, returns to the stand, and Daniel Penny could also testify at his New York City subway chokehold trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clonazepam tablets recalled over 'life-threatening' label mistake
item.description: The company that produces the tablets has said the medication is mislabeled with the incorrect strength on the carton.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canoeist is paddling the 6,000-mile Great Loop out of gratitude for life
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Peter Frank has paddled from Michigan's Upper Peninsula in June to the Chesapeake Bay in Maryland this month in his 1982 Sawyer Loon decked canoe, but he’s still got a long way to go.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No charges filed against man arrested after vigilante teens set up sting
item.description: A 46-year-old California man ambushed by vigilante teens after he allegedly provided a girl with nude photos of himself and tried to arrange a sexual encounter with her is, so far, not facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: St. Louis was once known as Mound City for its many Native American mounds. Just one remains
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — What is now St. Louis was once home to more than 100 mounds constructed by Native Americans — so many that St. Louis was once known as “Mound City.” Settlers tore most of them down, and just one remains.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pamela Hayden, longtime 'Simpsons' voice actor, hangs up her mic
item.description: “It’s been an honor and a joy to have worked on such a funny, witty, and groundbreaking show,” Hayden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, KRON4

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly $100 million awarded to the family of a man fatally shot in his apartment by an officer
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The family of a man shot and killed by a Dallas police officer who said she mistook his apartment for her own was awarded nearly $100 million Wednesday in a federal civil trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions testified Wednesday that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unhappy with the 2024 election results? This Italian village is offering $1 homes to Americans
item.description: Americans who are unhappy with the 2024 election results and have fantasized about leaving the country could turn that dream into a reality thanks to a recent offer from an Italian village.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments next year remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, ABC 4

item.title: Man convicted of killing nursing student Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Almost 100 House Democrats push for Gaetz Ethics report’s release
item.description: Nearly 100 House Democrats wrote to the House Ethics Committee, urging them to publish its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and their investigation into his alleged misconduct after President-elect Trump nominated him to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Adriana Vance collected herself in front of the cameras, two years to the day after her son was killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs, a murder that lawsuits allege could have been prevented by law enforcement and the club's owners.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA administrator says she supports investigation of alleged Trump bias in relief efforts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency told lawmakers Tuesday she has encouraged the agency's inspector general to review whether an employee was acting alone when directing workers helping hurricane victims not to go to homes with yards signs supporting President-elec
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5
